task_completion.description: JStock application is open and the 'Tech' watchlist is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible, and 'Tech' is selected in the watchlist dropdown or tab interface.

task_completion.description: Stock 'F' is added to the 'Tech' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ticker symbol 'F' is visible as an entry within the 'Tech' watchlist data grid.

task_completion.description: Stock 'GM' is added to the 'Tech' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ticker symbol 'GM' is visible as an entry within the 'Tech' watchlist data grid.

task_completion.description: The 'Tech' watchlist contains all expected unique stocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Tech' watchlist displays exactly five unique stocks: AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, F, and GM, with no duplicates.
The 'Auto' watchlist is no longer visible in the watchlist selection dropdown, watchlist manager, or tab interface.

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock GUI to modify the watchlists.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with JStock's search bar, add buttons, or watchlist manager, rather than a text editor modifying XML/CSV files.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to delete the watchlist.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show commands like 'rm' being used on the /home/ga/.jstock/1.0.7/UnitedState/watchlist directory.

integrity.description: The watchlist displays genuine application data grids.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stocks in the 'Tech' watchlist appear in the standard JStock table format with appropriate columns (e.g., Symbol, Last Price), not as plain text pasted into an arbitrary window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target watchlist is named 'Tech' and the source watchlist is named 'Auto'. The final consolidated 'Tech' watchlist must contain exactly these 5 unique ticker symbols: AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, F, GM. The 'Auto' watchlist must be completely deleted from the application.
